name: Henri Bergson
core_thesis: Article-backed thinker stub created during primitives_v2 Phase 2 from `data/articles/source/bergson.md`.

cross_engagements.brief: Abhinavagupta presses the same substrate structure axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `one-self-standing` rather than `process-field-with-no-enduring-substrate`.
Adi Sankara presses the same selfhood structure axis in the phenomenological register, but with the value `witness-self` rather than `psychic-individual`.
Akshapada Gautama presses the same method axis in the methodological register, but with the value `formal-proof-or-inference` rather than `mixed-or-layered`.
Gaudapada (Samkhya commentator) presses the same temporal mode axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `substance-primary` rather than `process-primary`.
G. W. F. Hegel presses the same evolution register axis in the historical-genealogical register, but with the value `sublative-becoming` rather than `durational-creative-growth`.
Akalanka presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `isolation-or-discriminative-release` rather than `not-soteriological`.

engaged_works.title: Matière et mémoire
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored modern monograph; bibliographic ascription is uncontroversial.
engaged_works.summary: Bergson's second major work, on the relation between body and mind, perception and memory. The text develops the doctrine of "pure memory" as a non-spatial, durational reality distinct from the cerebrally-instantiated motor habits Bergson attributes to the body, and argues that perception is selective rather than constitutive — the brain limits and channels a wider field of images rather than producing experience from sensory data. The work is the principal early articulation of Bergsonian *durée* and the durational theory of consciousness that Bergson later extends to cosmology in *L'Évolution créatrice*. It is read in the Vedāntic-Sāṃkhya comparative corpus as a parallel to the *antaḥkaraṇa* / *vāsanā* / *smṛti* tripartite analysis: pure memory plays a role structurally analogous to *saṃskāra* / *vāsanā* in the Yoga and Sāṃkhya traditions, and the selectivity-of-perception thesis runs parallel to the *vṛtti-nirodha* analysis of Yoga-Sūtra 1.2.

engaged_works.title: L'Évolution créatrice
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored modern monograph; bibliographic ascription is uncontroversial.
engaged_works.summary: Bergson's late masterwork, in which the durational analysis of consciousness is extended into a metaphysics of life. The central concept is *élan vital* — the creative impulse driving evolution non-mechanically, non-finalistically, through divergent lines (the plant, the arthropod, the vertebrate, the human). Bergson criticizes both Darwinian mechanism and Spencerian finalism as variants of the same intellectual error: the spatialization of time, the substitution of completed states for the durational creative act. The work is the principal twentieth-century vitalist metaphysics outside the Anglo-American tradition and is one of the explicit interlocutors of Aurobindo's *Life Divine* (Aurobindo cites and engages Bergson in the supramental-evolution chapters). The Vedāntic-comparative frame reads the *élan vital* as structurally analogous — though metaphysically not identical — to the *prāṇa* / *cit-śakti* / *parā-prakṛti* lineages in the Sāṃkhya and Tantric traditions.
